description_long: This object is a large, mounted anti-tank or anti-aircraft rifle, reminiscent of the German 8.8 cm Flak gun from World War II. It features a long, heavy barrel mounted on a robust, angular base with four stabilizing legs extending outward for support. The central body includes a complex arrangement of gears, adjustment wheels, and sighting mechanisms, indicating its use for precision targeting. Ammunition shells are visible on the side, and the gun is equipped with a prominent optical sighting device on top. The entire structure is constructed from dark green metal, with some smaller components in black and brown, giving it a military appearance. This weapon is designed for stationary deployment and would dominate any urban environment with its imposing size and industrial build.
description: A large, mounted anti-tank or anti-aircraft rifle with a long barrel, heavy metal base, and visible sighting and adjustment mechanisms. Its dark green military finish and robust construction make it a formidable stationary weapon.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the main body, and the stabilizing legs, with visible sighting and adjustment mechanisms.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel's muzzle, symmetrical stabilizing legs, and the central mounting structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the ammunition storage, adjustment wheels, and the full length of the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the breech, sighting device, and the mounting base, with the legs extending outward.
